notes: Roman noblewoman of the Julian family, noted for discretion and fair behavior; mother of Geta and stepmother to Antony.

docs.title: Life of Macrinus
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: … Sprung from a father corrupt, though virtuous truly his mother; Lovers a hundred she knew and a hundred were those whom she courted.⁠ 66 Lover was also the bald-head, who later was known as her husband; Pius indeed, aye Marcus indeed, for ne'er was he Verus.

docs.title: Life of Antony
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … Caesars, who, for her discretion and fair behavior, was not inferior to any of her time. Under her, Antony received his education, she being, after the death of his father, remarried to Cornelius Lentulus. who was put to death by Cicero for having been of Catiline’s conspiracy.
